title: Front Desk Coordinator
description:
The Front Desk Coordinator plays a key role in supporting the smooth day-to-day operations of the clinic and delivering an exceptional client experience. This position is the first point of contact for clients and supports the clinical team through effective administration, scheduling, and communication. The role works closely with clinicians and the clinic leadership team to ensure efficient operations and a welcoming clinic environment.

The Front Desk Coordinator is an essential part of the ATHOS team and actively contributes to providing a professional, friendly, and seamless experience for all clients.

Key Responsibilities

  • Deliver a warm, professional, and consistent client experience across all front desk interactions, from enquiries and bookings through to follow-up

  • Support daily clinic operations, including appointment scheduling, practitioner diaries, and workflow coordination

  • Manage administrative tasks such as client records, documentation, correspondence, and data entry in line with clinic standards

  • Process payments, manage invoices, and support basic financial administration as required

  • Assist with clinic organisation, including stock levels, supplies, and maintaining a clean and professional front desk environment

  • Support clinicians and clinic leadership with ad-hoc administrative and operational tasks

Role Requirements

  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage professionally with clients, clinicians, and team members

  • Highly organised and detail-oriented, able to manage multiple tasks and priorities in a busy clinic environment

  • Demonstrates professionalism, discretion, and integrity when handling confidential client and clinic information

  • Calm, reliable, and collaborative, with confidence using clinic systems and digital tools

A personal interest in health, fitness, or sports is a plus, as it aligns well with the clinic environment and client needs.

Role Details

  • Clinic-based role located at 321 Orchard Road (Somerset)

  • Full-time position (40 hours per week), Monday to Friday, 9:00am – 6:00pm

  • Start date: January 2026

Please note that this is a full-time, permanent position. We are seeking committed candidates who are keen to join the ATHOS team and contribute on a long-term basis.

postedCompany.description: ATHOS Performance is a physiotherapy and performance training clinic, dedicated to helping individuals of all backgrounds improve their health and well-being. Our clinic focuses on evidence-based practices and personalised care, utilising exercise and manual therapy as fundamental tools for rehabilitation, injury prevention, and sport performance optimisation.
postedCompany.name: ATHOS PERFORMANCE PTE. LTD.
